checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=256592128.72 (2016-12-31) vs 178690826.85 (2015-12-31), +43.6% growth vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth of +21.5% (1914679556.92 vs 1576466748.87)
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=331889536.72 (2018-03-31) vs 259291211.63 (2017-03-31), +28% growth while SalesRevenueGoodsNet Q1 2018=465726962.52 vs Q1 2017=442646713.07, +5.2% growth

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth FY2015 vs FY2014 only +24% (1576466748.87 vs 1270987841.47)
LongTermDebt=275677323.39 (2015-12-31) vs 117111765.30 (2014-12-31), reflecting debt-funded acquisition driving the goodwill jump

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2017 income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet FY2017=1965920924.88 vs FY2016=1914679556.92, only +2.7% growth
GrossProfit=922518930.42 / Revenue=1965920924.88 = 46.9% margin (FY2017) vs GrossProfit=785225854.34 / Revenue=1576466748.99 = 49.8% margin (FY2015)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly swung to a loss of -36230346.23 (Q4 2017) and -12462757.80 (Q1 2018) after years of growth, and quarterly OCF alternates between large negative Q1 figures (e.g. -52552586.56 in Q1 2016) and large positive Q3 figures (e.g. 82316259.79 annual 2012 vs quarterly swings)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=231217864.83 (2017-12-31) vs 168817952.42 (2016-12-31), +36.9% growth, far exceeding revenue growth of +2.7% for the same period

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2013-02-25 followed immediately by a 10-K/A filed 2013-02-26 (one day later)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided from 2007-12-31 through 2018-03-31 across Assets, Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, cash flow items, and balance sheet items

affected_line_items: Allowance for doubtful accounts (not separately reported)
mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature revenue recognition or extension of unusually lenient credit/payment terms to customers to accelerate reported sales, inflating receivables disproportionately relative to revenue growth (e.g., channel stuffing near period-end). This would overstate revenue and receivables and understate any related bad-debt reserve.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=178690826.85 (2015-12-31) grew +55% vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth of only +24% (1576466748.87 vs 1270987841.47) for FY2015
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=331889536.72 (2018-03-31) grew +28% YoY while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only +5.2% for the same quarter

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible failure to record adequate inventory write-downs/obsolescence reserves as inventory grew far faster than sales (26% vs 2.7% in FY2017) while gross margin compressed from 49.8% (2015) to 46.9% (2017), suggesting excess/aging inventory not yet reflected in cost of sales, which would overstate inventory and understate COGS in the near term with reversal risk in future periods.

Operating cash flow may be artificially supported by stretching payment terms to suppliers (accounts payable growing much faster than revenue), masking underlying earnings deterioration, as evidenced by a positive OCF in FY2017 despite a reported net loss.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=231217864.83 (2017-12-31) vs 168817952.42 (2016-12-31), +36.9% growth vs revenue growth of only +2.7%

top_signals: Accounts receivable growing materially faster than revenue in 2015, 2016, and Q1 2018 (e.g., +55% AR growth vs +24% revenue growth in FY2015; +28% AR growth vs +5.2% revenue growth in Q1 2018)

Persistent and severe divergence between net income and operating cash flow, including OCF of only $5.99M against net income of $95.8M in FY2015, and positive OCF of $96.5M despite a net loss of -$19.9M in FY2017
Inventory growing 26.3% in FY2017 while revenue grew only 2.7%, accompanied by gross margin compression from 49.8% to 46.9%, suggesting potential unrecognized inventory impairment risk
Accounts payable growing far faster than revenue (36.9% vs 2.7% in FY2017), a possible lever inflating reported operating cash flow amid weakening earnings
Rapid goodwill growth (+375% in FY2015) from acquisitions funded by debt, raising integration/impairment and purchase accounting risk
A same-week 10-K/10-K/A filing pair (2013-02-25 and 2013-02-26) indicating a rapid correction to originally filed financial statements
Company swung from consistent multi-year profit growth to a full-year net loss in FY2017 and continued losses into Q1 2018, coinciding with the working-capital anomalies noted above
